epic.name: Slack Shift Handoff Notifications
epic.objective: Automatically notify the incoming and outgoing on-call users via Slack DM when a shift handoff occurs, and post a summary to a configurable channel.
epic.definition_of_done: At every scheduled shift boundary, the incoming on-call user receives a Slack DM with upcoming shift details, and the outgoing user receives a handoff summary.

tasks.name: Shift notification worker
tasks.description: Background worker that watches for upcoming shift handoffs and sends notifications at the right time
acceptance_criteria: ShiftNotifier worker starts at application boot alongside the Slack socket-mode handler
On startup and every 1 minute: query all schedules, compute next handoff time for each layer
When current time crosses a handoff boundary: send DM to incoming user, send DM to outgoing user
Deduplication: track last-notified handoff timestamp per layer to avoid duplicate sends on restart
Graceful shutdown: worker stops cleanly on SIGTERM
Unit tests: next handoff computation, dedup logic
tasks.implementation_notes: Use a simple ticker (1-minute poll) rather than scheduling exact timers — simpler and resilient to restarts. Store last_notified_handoff per layer in the DB (or in-memory with a grace window). No Redis needed. The worker shares the ChatService interface so it works without Slack configured (graceful no-op).

tasks.name: Shift notification Slack messages
tasks.description: Format and send Slack DMs for shift handoffs
acceptance_criteria: Outgoing user DM: 'Your on-call shift for [Schedule Name] has ended. [Incoming User] is now on call.'
Incoming user DM: 'You are now on call for [Schedule Name] until [end_time]. Good luck!'
If schedule has a notification_channel configured: post a shift-change summary to that channel
Messages use Slack Block Kit for clean formatting
User lookup: map participant user_name to Slack user ID via users.lookupByEmail or users.list
Graceful fallback: if user lookup fails, log warning and skip DM (don't crash)
tasks.implementation_notes: Add optional notification_channel field to Schedule model (migration addendum or separate migration). User lookup can be cached for the duration of the worker's run to avoid rate limits. Block Kit message: header with schedule name, section with shift window, context with 'Powered by OpenIncident'.
